title: Securities Lending - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York operates a securities lending program authorized by the FOMC, lending Treasury and agency debt securities from the SOMA portfolio to primary dealers via daily competitive bidding auctions at noon. The program is voluntary for primary dealers and promotes smooth clearing of Treasury and agency securities in support of monetary policy implementation.
key_points: NY Fed lends Treasury and agency debt securities from the SOMA portfolio to primary dealers on a temporary basis
Loans are awarded through competitive bidding in a multiple price auction held each business day at noon
Participation by primary dealers is entirely voluntary
The program promotes smooth clearing of Treasury and agency debt securities
Summary results are released publicly following each day's auction
Transaction data is published quarterly, approximately two years after the transaction, per Dodd-Frank Act requirements
A statement regarding Open Market Desk Operations on Friday, April 3, 2026 was issued on March 12, 2026
why_it_matters: The NY Fed's securities lending program is a key component of Treasury market plumbing, allowing dealers to source securities for delivery obligations while ensuring market liquidity and efficient settlement. This supports the smooth functioning of the $25+ trillion Treasury market, which is foundational to broader financial markets and monetary policy transmission.
market_structure_context: The securities lending program is part of the NY Fed's market operations infrastructure. It provides temporary access to Treasuries from the SOMA portfolio, supporting repo and settlement markets. The daily noon auction and public release of results provide transparency into dealer demand for securities.
